seoTitle: Edit and Correct an Invoice in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Fix a typo, missed discount, or wrong tax calculation on a live invoice in QuickBooks Online without deleting the transaction.
intro: A mistake on a sent invoice doesn't mean you have to start over. This guide shows how to locate, edit, and correct a live invoice in QuickBooks Online, from fixing line items to updating discounts and tax, then resending the corrected version.

steps.heading: Locate the invoice
steps.body: Go to Sales, then Invoices, and use the search bar or date filters to find the target document.

steps.heading: Open it for editing
steps.body: Click the Edit option in the Action column next to the invoice you want to change.

steps.heading: Update customer and terms
steps.body: Adjust the billing address, payment terms, or invoice date in the header fields if needed.

steps.heading: Correct line items
steps.body: Scroll to the Product/Service table and rewrite descriptions, quantities, or rates to let QuickBooks recalculate subtotals.

steps.heading: Adjust discounts and tax, then resend
steps.body: Apply any discounts or tax changes at the bottom, add a note explaining the correction, and click Save and Send to deliver the updated invoice.

faq.question: Can I edit an invoice that already has a partial payment applied?
faq.answer: You can, but it's important to review carefully since changing amounts on a partially paid invoice can create balance discrepancies.

faq.question: Will editing an invoice notify my client automatically?
faq.answer: No, you need to click Save and Send to resend the corrected version so the client sees the updated document.

faq.question: Can I change the tax calculation on an existing invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, adjusting quantities, rates, or tax toggles in the summary section will let QuickBooks recalculate the tax automatically.

faq.question: Should I tell the client what changed?
faq.answer: It's good practice to add a note in the message box explaining the correction before resending.
